content:
Andrej Karpathy Said He Had Never Felt So Behind as a Programmer. Then One Developer Did Something About It.

A single 70-line file on GitHub just crossed 220,000 stars. Here is what it says about the state of AI coding tools that every engineer needs to understand.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Aleh Tsikhanau on UnsplashOn January 26, 2026, Andrej Karpathy posted something on X that stopped engineers mid-scroll.
Karpathy is not a person who says things like this casually. He co-founded OpenAI. He built Tesla’s Autopilot vision system. He coined the term vibe coding. When he says he has never felt so behind as a programmer, engineers pay attention.
What he described was specific. Over the previous two months his coding workflow had flipped from 80 percent manual coding with autocomplete to 80 percent agent-driven coding with the remaining 20 percent devoted to edits and cleanup. He called it the biggest change in his programming workflow in roughly two decades.
Then he named something the rest of the industry had been experiencing but nobody had said clearly enough to stick.
The AI coding agents were failing in specific, reproducible, maddening ways. And he named exactly what those ways were.
